summary: Cooling demand is running well above normal (+11.7% over the next week, +20.1% over 8-16 days, both near the top of their 5y range), and seasonality tilts increasingly higher into the 3-4 week window (56%/50%/56%/63% odds of higher prices) with September historically the strongest month. Fair value itself is rising ($2.93 now to $3.10 by +21 days), so the front's current +$0.32 premium fades toward roughly fair over the coming three weeks even without a rally. Managed money is extremely net short (18th percentile of the 5y range), which is contrarian-bullish fuel for a short-covering move, though a +175 Bcf storage surplus versus the 5y average and a bearish weekly surprise are a persistent headwind. Short-term momentum looks stretched (5m/15m RSI 83.8/74.1, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h), arguing against chasing strength right here even though the 4h trend remains bullish. Net: a constructive fundamental backdrop for the next several weeks, but tactically due for a pause or pullback over the next few days.

longTerm.entry: $3.00 - $3.15 (buy a pullback toward the plus7D fair-value zone)
longTerm.rationale: Cooling demand is +12%/+20% vs 5y (1-7D/8-16D) near the top of its range, seasonality favors upside into weeks 3-4 (63% historically higher, Sept the strongest month), fair value rises from $2.93 to $3.10 over the next 3 weeks, and managed-money shorts are near a 5y extreme (18th percentile) that could unwind into a squeeze.
longTerm.invalidation: A close below $2.85 alongside 8-16D cooling demand rolling back toward the 5y norm and continued bearish storage surprises would invalidate the long case.

shortTerm.rationale: 5m/15m technicals are extremely overbought (RSI 83.8/74.1, price above the upper Bollinger band on 5m/15m/1h) right after the front traded to a +$0.32 premium over today's fair value, arguing against chasing strength here; but strong 8-16D cooling demand and a bullish 4h trend argue against shorting into it either.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a pullback into the $3.00-3.15 fair-value zone with 15m/1h RSI cooling below ~60 to buy, or (b) a confirmed break below $3.10 with another bearish storage surprise to consider a tactical short.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand +12%/+20% vs 5y (1-7D/8-16D), near top of historical range
Managed money net short at 18th 5y percentile - short-covering fuel
Fair value trending up from $2.93 now to $3.10 by +21 days
Storage surplus +175 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish weekly surprise (+13 Bcf vs forecast)
Short-term technicals extremely overbought (RSI >74 on 5m/15m, price above upper Bollinger)
risks: Seasonality reliability is LOW (51pp 10-90% spread) - weak statistical edge this week
Growing storage surplus could keep expanding and cap rallies if production stays high
Overbought short-term momentum could unwind sharply, especially around the next storage report
